stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Visions in Bloom was a 2019 exhibition at the Brooklyn Museum in New York.
  • It featured ten artworks by Ghanaian artist Akosua Mensah.
  • One of these works is a painting titled Threads of Memory.
  • Threads of Memory depicts four seated figures.
  • The figures wear scarves made of woven kente cloth strips stitched to the painting's canvas.
stem: The student wants to describe how kente cloth is used in Threads of Memory. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: In Threads of Memory, the figures' scarves are made of woven kente cloth strips stitched to the painting's canvas.
The exhibition Visions in Bloom featured artworks by artist Akosua Mensah.
Depicting four seated, scarf-wearing figures, Threads of Memory is a painting by Akosua Mensah.
Akosua Mensah's Threads of Memory was one of ten artworks exhibited in Visions in Bloom at the Brooklyn Museum.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. This choice directly describes how kente cloth is used in Threads of Memory, by explaining that the kente cloth strips are part of the painting itself.

Choice B is incorrect. This choice provides contextual information about the exhibition, but it doesn't mention anything about the painting or the kente cloth. Choice C is incorrect. This choice mentions that the figures wear scarves, but it doesn't explain how the kente cloth is integrated into the painting. Choice D is incorrect. This choice provides contextual information about the painting, but it doesn't mention anything about the kente cloth or how it is used.
